piec
  • API Reference
  • piec.drivers
  • piec.drivers.z_old
  • piec.drivers.z_old.mccdig
  • View page source

piec.drivers.z_old.mccdig

Set’s up the digilent instrument class that uses wrappers to the mcculw library Realistically this should be setup like the SCPI classes and then you have subclasses for the DAQ model. However it seems that mcculw does a good job of autodetecting abiltiies so may just be worth it to have a single one that just uses the paramters from the original probe to the instrument in the __init__ file

Functions

add_data(data, board_num, data_array, ...)

My version of add_example_data

add_example_data(board_num, data_array, ...)

config_device([use_device_detection, ...])

Configures the device and returns the accepted params from the device :param use_device_detection: True or False :type use_device_detection: bool :param dev_id_list: :type dev_id_list: list

config_first_detected_device(board_num[, ...])

Adds the first available device to the UL.

get_sparse_array(arr)

Taken with help from Deepseek V3 Deepthink Used to re sparse an array from a dense array

initialize_built_in_functions()

is_device_connected(board_num)

make_sin_wave(freq)

Classes

MCC_DAQ([address])

Custom Class for using an MCC DAQ NOTE: Currently relies on only one MCC DAQ being plugged in at a time.

Waveform_holder(name, data, channel)

This is a helper class that is used to store the arb waveforms in memory so you have access to more than just volatile.
